text: I don't think anyone else would really remember the days, 1920s, when Jacobius was basically doing a lot of thoracoscopies.
Those were really, really early days of thoracoscopy.
Most of us would be familiar with this kind of setting in the OT room.
Even this kind of setting is thought to be outdated, of course, now with the much nicer HD TVs and camera units.

And in that paper, he used really very small incisions, two 2.5-centimeter incisions in those days, and with a fifth or sixth intercostal space, and did wedges for lingular and also for middle lobe lung pathologies or nodules.
